text.title: cohort-implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cohort-implementation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using DATEDIFF without specifying the warehouse; BigQuery and Snowflake/Redshift have opposite argument order.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silent wrong results — the query runs but returns incorrect day offsets. Always specify the warehouse in agent prompts and validate output against a known cohort week.

failure-mode.description: Using the schedule + while True refresh loop from notebook examples in production code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A long-running Python process with sleep is not reliable for production. Use Airflow DAG, Cloud Scheduler, or dbt Cloud scheduled runs instead.

failure-mode.description: Joining on user PII (email, name) instead of user_id in cohort queries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PII joins bypass anonymization controls and create GDPR compliance risk. Always join on opaque user_id.
